summary: Antiphospholipid antibodies are not linked to avascular necrosis of bone in systemic lupus erythematosus; while they contribute to thrombosis, their association with osteonecrosis remains unsupported by current literature. This indicates that such antibodies do not play a recognized role in causing avascular necrosis in SLE patients.
